excluded_defs.reason: read to confirm the property name and type on ValidationFailure, but no changes needed there

exploration_log.aha_moment: The `ToString(string separator)` method that does the actual work just needs a conditional: if failure.Severity != Severity.Error, prefix with [severity]. One-line change.
exploration_log.hindsight: Nothing to change about the approach - it was immediately clear where the fix goes.

queries.query_text: Fix ValidationResult.ToString() in ValidationResult.cs to prefix each message with [Warning] or [Info] when failure severity is not the default Error

solve_notes: Updated `ValidationResult.ToString(string separator)` to prefix each message with `[{failure.Severity}]` when the severity is not the default `Severity.Error`. Both `ToString()` overloads are updated (the parameterless one just calls the separator overload, which now handles severity).
task_text:
The `ValidationResult` class in `Results/ValidationResult.cs` has a
`ToString()` method that joins error messages, but it does not include
the `Severity` level (Error, Warning, Info) of each `ValidationFailure`.
Fix `ToString()` to prefix each message with its severity when the
severity is not the default `Error`, e.g., `"[Warning] Name is too short"`.
